source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.454 (scan idx 453), TABULA XVII
family: labyrinthus-radialis-quincunx (FIVE connected polar wheels in a cross/quincunx, joined by ET / fovet / AVSTRIACOS scroll)
title_diplomatic: Reverendissimo & Eximio D. Mag. mo D. MICHAELI ... dignissimo apud S. Crucem Abbati Cisterciensis Ordinis Visitatori et Vicario Generali, S. Theol. Doctori ... anno MDCLIII ... Monsserratenses (Claustra Sancta Crucis) Studiosi.

author_combinatorial_claim.diplomatic: novies mille sexcenta et quadraginta quatuor billiones (seu millionum milliones) centies decies septies mille & quadringenti triginta duo milliones, septingenta quindecim millia sexcenta et octo carmina
author_combinatorial_claim.note: ~9.644 QUADRILLION carmina — the LARGEST asserted multiplicity in the entire book. Five wheels whose joint permutation yields the figure. The supreme adversarial/synthetic-ambiguity calibration target.

figure.emblem_layer_note: Two bishops (mitres) flank a crucifix medallion (left) and a stork medallion (right); central scroll reads ET / fovet / AVSTRIACOS.

calibration_role: SUPREME adversarial calibration target: asserted |Σ| ~ 9.6e15 (quadrillions). If the synthetic-ambiguity detector saturates here, it is behaving correctly — legitimate but maximally inflated.
